phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest Federal Reserve policy data and DXY component central bank stances. Fetch and report: - Fed current policy stance (Hawkish / Neutral / Dovish) + next FOMC meeting rate-change probability (from CME FedWatch — exact %) - Fed Terminal Rate Expectation: where is the Fed Funds rate expected to settle by year-end? (from CME FedWatch year-end strip or consensus forecasts — exact %) - Any Fed speaker comments in the last 48 hours (note name + hawkish/dovish tone) - ECB current stance + ECB Deposit Facility Rate year-end expectation (as the EUR has a ~57.6% weight in DXY — a more dovish ECB = DXY bullish pressure; exact %) - BOE current stance + BOE Bank Rate year-end expectation (GBP has ~11.9% weight in DXY; exact %) - JPY (BoJ) brief stance note (JPY has ~13.6% weight in DXY) - Net policy divergence signal: Is the Fed more hawkish relative to the ECB and BOE? Does this support a higher or lower DXY? - Overall DXY Policy Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] with one-line justification ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Fed Stance: [Hawkish/Neutral/Dovish] | Next FOMC Cut/Hike Probability: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - ECB Stance: [Hawkish/Dovish] | Year-End Rate: [X%] | DXY Weight Impact: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral for DXY] - BOE Stance: [Hawkish/Dovish] | Year-End Rate: [X%] | DXY Weight Impact: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral for DXY] - BoJ Stance: [brief note] | DXY Weight Impact: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral for DXY] - Fed vs Peers Divergence: [Widening/Narrowing/Flat] — net effect on DXY - Key Fed Speaker Note: [name + comment in last 48h, or None] - DXY Policy Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - FED POLICY & DXY WEIGHTING] - Fed Outlook: [stance + next FOMC probability % + year-end terminal rate %] - ECB (57.6% weight): [stance + year-end rate % + DXY impact] - BOE (11.9% weight): [stance + year-end rate % + DXY impact] - BoJ (13.6% weight): [stance + DXY impact] - Fed vs Peers Divergence: [Widening/Narrowing/Flat] - Key Fed Speaker Note: [name + tone, or None] - Core Logic: [why the Fed-vs-peers rate gap matters for DXY direction today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Fetch and report (only confirmed released data): - US CPI (headline and core) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not yet released). Is it a beat or miss? What does it imply for Fed rate expectations? - US NFP (Non-Farm Payrolls) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not yet released). Beat = USD bullish; Miss = USD bearish - US Retail Sales — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not yet released). A consumer spending pulse - ISM Manufacturing PMI and ISM Services PMI — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not yet released) - Citigroup US Economic Surprise Index (exact value — positive = US data beating expectations; state "Data Unavailable" if not found) - Any high-impact US data releases scheduled TODAY that could spike DXY volatility 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - CPI (Headline/Core): [Actual % vs Forecast % vs Previous %] | Beat/Miss | DXY Impact: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - NFP: [Actual K vs Forecast K vs Previous K] | Beat/Miss | DXY Impact: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - Retail Sales: [Actual % vs Forecast % vs Previous %] | Beat/Miss | DXY Impact: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - ISM Manufacturing PMI: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | Above/Below 50 | DXY Impact: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - ISM Services PMI: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | Above/Below 50 | DXY Impact: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - Citi US Economic Surprise Index: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Reading: [Positive/Negative = US outperforming/underperforming] - Today's Scheduled Risk: [event name + expected DXY volatility impact, or None] - US Data Momentum Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Mixed / Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - US ECONOMIC DATA MOMENTUM] - CPI: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | Beat/Miss | DXY Impact: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - NFP: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | Beat/Miss | DXY Impact: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - Retail Sales: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | Beat/Miss | DXY Impact: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - ISM Manufacturing PMI: [value] | ISM Services PMI: [value] - Citi US Surprise Index: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Reading: [Positive/Negative] - Today's Scheduled Risk: [event name or None] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [is US data momentum accelerating or slowing — what it means for Fed policy and DXY demand] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

📊 FX OPTIONS EXPIRIES CHECK — DXY (MANDATORY FOR FOREX SENTIMENT): Browse for any large FX options expiries or option barrier cuts scheduled for TODAY [CURRENT_DATE] at key strike prices for DXY. The two primary cut times are: (1) Tokyo Cut: 02:00 UTC — primarily used for Asian-session options. (2) New York Cut (NY Cut): 15:00 UTC — the dominant global cut time, where the largest notional options expire. SOURCES: Check DTCC swap data repository, Reuters IFR Markets options commentary, CME Group FX options data, or Bloomberg FX options scanner. LOGIC: — Large open interest at a key strike acts as a PRICE MAGNET in the hours leading up to the cut — dealers hedge their gamma exposure by pushing price toward the strike. — Once a large strike expires, the 'gravitational pull' disappears and price can move freely — this is often a source of sharp post-cut volatility. — A large option BARRIER (knock-in or knock-out) at a key level can make price action 'sticky' near that barrier — it acts as a WALL rather than a magnet. If no significant expiries are found, state: 'No significant DXY options expiries noted for today.' ⚠ ANTI-HALLUCINATION: Do NOT fabricate strikes or notional sizes. If you cannot find confirmed data, state 'Options expiry data unavailable — check IFR Markets or CME for confirmation.' Output additions: - DXY NY Cut Options (15:00 UTC): [strike level + notional size in USD/EUR millions, or 'No significant expiry'] (Source: [domain]) | Effect: [Price Magnet at [level] / Barrier Wall at [level] / No notable effect] - DXY Tokyo Cut Options (02:00 UTC): [strike level + notional, or 'No significant expiry'] | Effect: [Magnet / Wall / None] - Options Expiry Directional Bias Implication: [Bullish DXY — magnet above / Bearish DXY — magnet below / Neutral — no dominant expiry] Add to compressed summary block: - Options Expiries (DXY): [NY Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | [Tokyo Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | Bias Implication: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - CFTC Net USD Positioning: [contracts + long/short] | Extreme? [Yes/No] | Macro Context: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral trend] - VIX: [level] | Direction: [Up/Down] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On / Risk-Off] - S&P 500: [direction + %] | Equity-USD Correlation Signal: [Safe-Haven USD bid / Risk-On USD headwind / Decoupled] - Dollar Smile Region: [Left/Bottom/Right] | Reason: [one line explanation] - CNN Fear & Greed: [level + reading] - Overall Sentiment Bias for DXY: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P4 - SENTIMENT, POSITIONING & DOLLAR SMILE] - CFTC Net USD: [contracts + long/short + extreme? Yes/No] | Macro Context: [trend direction] - VIX: [level] | Direction: [Up/Down] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On / Risk-Off] - S&P 500: [direction %] | Equity Signal: [Safe-Haven bid / Risk-On headwind / Decoupled] - Dollar Smile Region: [Left (Risk-Off) / Bottom (Weak USD) / Right (US Growth)] | Logic: [why] - CNN Fear & Greed: [level + reading] - Core Logic: [which Smile driver is dominant today — risk-off panic or strong US growth outperformance?] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear DXY (US Dollar Index) institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Fed Policy & DXY Weighting: [P1_OUTPUT]
2. US Treasury Yields & Curve: [P2_OUTPUT]
3. US Economic Data Momentum: [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Sentiment, Positioning & Dollar Smile: [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Macro & Geopolitical Risk: [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict, lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Apply the Dollar Smile Theory: determine whether USD strength (if bullish) is driven by Risk-Off safe-haven demand (Left Smile) or US Economic Outperformance (Right Smile) — or whether the USD is in the weak Bottom Smile zone. State which is active.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this DXY bias today based on scheduled events and session liquidity.

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Dollar Smile Region: [Left (Risk-Off) / Bottom (Weak USD) / Right (US Growth)]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event or data shift would flip this bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., session + time UTC + reason]
